name: Alpha Base Book
tagline: Public read-only $2,500 cislunar book — locked Mondays, marked live
next_rebalance_note: Next public rebalance: Monday (educational lock). Intraday prices mark live; weights only change on Monday publish. Catalyst scoreboard rescored weekly with the book.
thesis: Seed book for X ops: launch cadence (RKLB) + connectivity (ASTS) + lunar delivery (LUNR) + Earth observation (PL) + hard-money crypto sleeve (BTC). Every name runs the Catalyst Scoreboard so the *why* is public. Replace seed shares with the live Monday lock before promoting hard on X.
disclaimer: Educational simulation only. Not financial advice, not a recommendation, not a solicitation. This is a public read-only view of a $2,500 paper book run for engagement and teaching. Catalyst scores are operator judgment for transparency — not ratings, not price targets. Verify prices yourself. Past performance does not predict future results.

week_open.note: Monday open mark for Friday P&L strip. Update each Monday lock.

catalyst_framework.name: Catalyst Scoreboard

entry_questions.question: Does it reduce cost or increase the reliability of getting mass to the lunar surface?
Does it create a recurring revenue stream once the base is operational (not just one-time launch revenue)?
Is there a clear customer with money already allocated (NASA, DoD, commercial constellation, hyperscale)?
Does the company control a bottleneck technology that is hard to substitute?
Is the timeline measurable in quarters, not in someday?

weekly_dimensions.label: Customer & capital
weekly_dimensions.question: Clear customer with money already allocated?

weekly_dimensions.label: Narrative · Moon Alpha Base
weekly_dimensions.question: Does the market understand why this matters for the base?

catalyst_framework.note: Entry gate: 5 checklist items (G=1, Y=0.5, R=0). Must total ≥3 to enter the book as a lunar-ops name. Weekly score: /10. Educational operator judgment only — not advice. Weekly rubric completes the stated 4+2+1 with customer capital (2) + timeline (1) so the card is a full 10.

catalyst.why: Cadence + Neutron path is the mass-to-orbit bridge; gov + commercial manifests keep the calendar real. Launch is partly one-shot revenue — recurring is thinner until constellation logistics stack.
catalyst.risks: Neutron execution risk; competition from larger launch supply.

catalyst.why: Recurring connectivity + spectrum/tech bottleneck if they lock commercial use. Weak on lunar mass logistics; base story is comms layer once surface ops need bandwidth.
catalyst.risks: Execution/timeline slippage; capital intensity; narrative often runs ahead of revenue.

catalyst.why: Direct lunar surface delivery (CLPS) — highest narrative fit for Moon Alpha Base logistics. NASA-aligned customer capital; mission cadence is quarter-visible.
catalyst.risks: Mission failure history; concentration in task orders; thin recurring until services stack.

catalyst.why: Recurring data revenue + real customers now. Weak direct mass-to-Moon link; base relevance is sensing / site intel / Earth–cislunar situational awareness.
catalyst.risks: Commodity EO pressure; lunar narrative is indirect; dilution/competition.

catalyst.why: Not a lunar-ops name. Hard-money ballast / optionality sleeve so the book is not 100% aerospace beta. Tracked for transparency — does not need entry gate ≥3.
catalyst.risks: Volatility; no lunar cash-flow story; narrative drift if oversized.

x_ops.cta: Clone this book into your free sim — build your own cislunar stack.
